goal: Become 100x more productive by spending more time on high-leverage strengths and less time manually coordinating low-leverage work.

optimizedBlocks.why: Choose the few outcomes that multiply the week before meetings fragment attention.
Use peak cognition for architecture, code review, and high-context implementation.
Batch local model and artifact experiments while working memory is fresh.
Convert research into decisions, not a larger reading queue.
Protect your strength in synthesizing systems before the design direction meeting.
Promote lessons, remove stale commitments, and set next-week defaults.
Compensate for open loops with one bounded owner/action pass.

team.job: Select the weekly few, rank by leverage, and reject low-yield commitments.
Turn schedule input into protected blocks, meeting clusters, and recovery buffers.
Maintain owner/action/date logs and draft follow-ups for approval.
Learn which block shapes create better output and detect fragmentation.
Flag uncertainty, missing schedule data, and overconfident productivity claims.

learningLedger.hypothesis: Protecting morning build blocks will improve leverage more than adding more tasks.
learningLedger.result: Deep work increases from 2.0 to 10.25 hours and context switches drop from 18 to 9.
learningLedger.acceptedLesson: Schedule high-context creation before coordination whenever fixed events allow it.

learningLedger.hypothesis: A single follow-up batch will reduce open-loop drag without consuming peak cognition.
learningLedger.result: Admin time is capped at two hours and follow-up work moves to Friday late morning.
learningLedger.acceptedLesson: Batch owner/action/date work after strategic decisions have been made.

learningLedger.hypothesis: A focus schedule strategy will improve output quality without silently rewriting preferences.
learningLedger.result: Learning review creates an explicit promotion gate for future schedule defaults and strategy selection.
learningLedger.acceptedLesson: Only promote productivity lessons when a scored week improves without new trust or health costs.

rules: No meeting may overwrite a protected block without an explicit tradeoff note.
The agent must say when schedule data is missing instead of inventing availability.
The agent may draft calendar changes but does not send invites or messages without approval.
Productivity claims must be tied to observable schedule metrics or labeled as hypotheses.
